title: To be rich you must predict the future, one way thru past insights (Sept 28 2022 — title-only manifesto)
html:
TL;DR

  • Sept 28, 2022 note that exists entirely as its title: "To be rich you must predict the future, one way thru past insights." The body is empty.
  • A pure specimen of Yahir's title-as-thesis capture pattern — the entire idea is encoded in the slug, no body content needed.
  • The maxim itself is canonical value investing reframed: predict the future is the goal, via past insights is the method (i.e., learn what happened in past cycles to recognize the next one). It's asking the right question applied to time-series.
  • Drops in the same fall-2022 window as the mom a house declaration (Sept 23) and the "6 things for OSIG" todo (Sept 20) — Yahir is in an active goal-and-thesis-formation week.
  • Pre-dates ChatGPT launch by ~9 weeks, so this isn't AI-thesis content — it's pure value-investor-Yahir at the OSIG desk.

Key quote

"To be rich you must predict the future, one way thru past insights"

Why it matters

This belongs in the value investing arc as the OSIG-period restatement of the Munger maxims Yahir was repeating two years earlier in 2020 04 23 go to diamond ("go where they ain't / circle of competence / if we knew the answer"). The vocabulary has shifted ("predict the future" instead of "circle of competence"), but the operation is the same: don't try to outrun the present; identify the structural thing the present hasn't priced in. That posture is what gets repointed at AI two months later in the Ahmad Jamal junk drawer.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir intend to come back and flesh this out into a longer piece? (The empty body suggests a placeholder.)
  • "One way thru past insights" — does Yahir mean one way among many, or the singular path? (Phrasing is ambiguous.)

Wikilinks
